title: Cannot mount a volume when using another WSL2 distribution
body:
### Issue Description

I have followed the instructions at https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/accessing-podman-from-another-wsl-instance, 

```
ls -lsrth /usr/local/bin/podman*
41M -rwxr-xr-x 1 alexiswl alexiswl 41M Feb  2 01:09 /usr/local/bin/podman-remote-static-linux_amd64
  0 lrwxrwxrwx 1 root     root      32 Apr  2 12:26 /usr/local/bin/podman -> podman-remote-static-linux_amd64
```

```
$ podman --version
podman version 4.9.1
```

```
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED      SIZE
docker.io/library/python  3-slim      23cd4350f4bd  11 days ago  135 MB
docker.io/library/ubuntu  latest      ca2b0f26964c  4 weeks ago  80.4 MB
```

```
$ podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                                 Identity    Default
podman-machine-default-root  unix:///wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock              true
```

*note the name is wrong above and because I mount my wsl2 distribution from / rather than `/mnt` `unix:///mnt/wsl` becomes `unix:///wsl`.  

I can also run and enter a container like so

```
podman run --rm -it ubuntu
root@f568beb07418:/# cat /etc/os-release
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="22.04"
VERSION="22.04.4 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)"
VERSION_CODENAME=jammy
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=jammy
````

However, when I run 

```
podman run --volume $PWD/inputs/:/data/inputs/ ubuntu:latest
```

I get

```
Error: statfs /home/alexiswl/cwltool-podman-test/inputs: no such file or directory
```

But this directory does exist `ls $PWD/inputs/` returns the list of files in this directory.  

How can I mount volumes from another WSL2 instance?


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Follow steps in `https://podman-desktop.io/docs/podman/accessing-podman-from-another-wsl-instance`
2. Check podman connection
3. Try mounting a volume like so `podman run --volume $PWD/inputs/:/data/inputs/ ubuntu:latest`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

I received the error message `Error: statfs /home/alexiswl/cwltool-podman-test/inputs: no such file or directory`

### Describe the results you expected

To run the container with the volume mounted	

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: volumes are always server side, so you must make sure you volume exists on the podman machine wsl instance not on your client.
